name: empty_contains_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_empty_contains_zero(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
    ensures
        cm@.contains(0),
{
}
reason: An empty set must not contain element 0; provability would mean create_empty's postcondition is unsound or Set::empty is mis-specified.

name: empty_contains_max_index
code:
proof fn phi_2_empty_contains_max_index(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
    ensures
        cm@.contains(511int),
{
}
reason: 511 is the maximum representable bit index (8*64-1); the empty set must not contain it, otherwise the boundary of the mask domain is broken.

name: empty_contains_negative
code:
proof fn phi_3_empty_contains_negative(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
    ensures
        cm@.contains(-1int),
{
}
reason: Negative indices are outside the valid bit range; if the empty set view contains -1, the view abstraction leaks invalid indices.

name: empty_has_element
code:
proof fn phi_4_empty_has_element(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
    ensures
        exists|i: int| cm@.contains(i),
{
}
reason: The empty set must have no elements; if an existential witness can be found, the spec of create_empty or the view is inconsistent with Set::empty.

name: empty_mask_nonzero
reason: If the view is empty the underlying mask word should be zero; provability would mean the abstraction allows non-canonical representations that silently hide set members.
